zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Diffee於1766年4月3日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡羅來納州蘭多夫時，她的父親William Diffee Jr33歲，母親Elizabeth Ann York40歲。她於1779年與John Wesley Sheffeild Jr在美國北卡羅來納州摩爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。她去世於1804年在美國北卡羅來納州蘭多夫享年38歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Diffee于1766年4月3日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡罗来纳州蘭多夫时，她的父亲William Diffee Jr33岁，母亲Elizabeth Ann York40岁。她于1779年与John Wesley Sheffeild Jr在美国北卡罗来纳州摩爾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子的父母。她去世于1804年在美国北卡罗来纳州蘭多夫享年38岁。

en.Randolph, North Carolina, British Coloni: Randolph, North Carolina, British Colonial America

nameMeanings.name: Diffee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Diffey  . Compare   Defee  .

History: William Diffee, from whom present-day American bearers of this name are descended, was born c. 1710, probably in Ireland, and died in 1790 in Randolph County, NC. His surname was also spelled Diffey, while his descendants also bear the surname Defee .

variants.name: Diffey
meanings: English (Dorset): perhaps a nickname from Anglo-Norman French defaé, defieu ‘faithless, disloyal’. Compare Diffee .

variants.name: Defee
meanings: History: This surname is borne by the descendants of William Diffee, born c. 1710, probably in Ireland, died 1790 in Randolph County, NC. The same spelling is recorded in London, England, in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: Tryon Palace
historicEvents.description: Built on August 26, 1767, the Tryon Palace became the capitol building for North Carolina. The building was named after William Tryon a British officer and colonial official.
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: A Free America
historicEvents.description: The Revolutionary War ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris which gave the new nation boundries on which they could expand and trade with other countries without any problems.

historicEvents.title: The Making of the U.S. Constitution.
historicEvents.description: The Philadelphia Convention was intended to be the first meeting to establish the first system of government under the Articles of Confederation. From this Convention, the Constitution of the United States was made and then put into place making it one of the major events in all American History.

historicEvents.title: Becomes 12th State
historicEvents.description: On November 21, 1789, North Carolina became the 12th state in the Union.

historicEvents.title: Gold Nuggets Found
historicEvents.description: "In 1799, in Little Meadow Creak located in Cabarrus County, North Carolina a large yellow """"rock"""" was found by Conrad Reed. A few years later it was determined that the """"rock"""" was a gold nugget."
historicEvents.urlTitle: Legends of America- Reed Gold Mine, North Carolina- First Gold Discovery

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Diffee was born on 3 April 1766, in Randolph, North Carolina, British Colonial America, her father, William Diffee Jr, was 33 and her mother, Elizabeth Ann York, was 40. She married John Wesley Sheffeild Jr in 1779, in Moore, North Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. She died in 1804, in Randolph, North Carolina, United States, at the age of 38.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Diffee est née le 3 avril 1766, à Randolph, Caroline du Nord, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, William Diffee Jr, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Ann York, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé John Wesley Sheffeild Jr en 1779, à Moore, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils. Elle est décédée en 1804, à Randolph, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis, à l’âge de 38 ans.

de.Randolph, North Carolina, British Coloni: Randolph, North Carolina, British Colonial America
de.lifeSummary: Als Sarah Diffee am 3. April 1766 in Randolph, North Carolina, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Diffee Jr 33 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Ann York 40. Sie hat 1779 in Moore, North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten John Wesley Sheffeild Jr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne. Sie starb 1804 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Randolph, North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten.

Lei sposato John Wesley Sheffeild Jr nel 1779, nel Moore, Carolina del Nord, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli. ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Diffeeが1766年4月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカノースカロライナ州ランドルフで生まれた時，父William Diffee Jrは33歳，母Elizabeth Ann Yorkは40歳であった。1779年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ムーアでJohn Wesley Sheffeild Jrと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子の親であった。彼女は1804年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ランドルフで38歳で死去した。

ko.Randolph, North Carolina, British Coloni: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 노스캐롤라이나 주 랜돌프
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Diffee(이)가 1766년 4월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 노스캐롤라이나 주 랜돌프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Diffee Jr(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Ann York(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 1779년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 무어에서 John Wesley Sheffeild Jr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1804년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 랜돌프에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Randolph, North Carolina, British Coloni: рандолф, Северная Каролина, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Diffee родилась 3 апреля 1766 г. в местности рандолф, Северная Каролина, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени William Diffee Jr было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Ann York, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Wesley Sheffeild Jr в 1779 г. в местности мур, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей. Она умерла в 1804 г. в местности рандолф, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 38 лет.
